hero.alt: Close-up of a seven-spotted ladybug with bright red wing covers and black spots
hero.caption: Ladybugs are beetles known for their shiny red dome-shaped bodies and black spots.

habitat.fileTitle: File:Siebenpunkt-Marienkäfer (Coccinella septempunctata) auf Blüte im FFH-Gebiet "Viernheimer Waldheide und angrenzende Flächen".jpg
habitat.alt: A ladybug walking on a yellow wildflower in a sun-drenched grassy meadow
habitat.caption: They live in grasslands, deciduous forests, agricultural fields, and home gardens.

diet.alt: A ladybug devouring tiny green aphids on a plant stem
diet.caption: Ladybugs are active carnivores that eat aphids and other plant-damaging pests.

baby.alt: A ladybug larva with a spiky blue-black body and orange spots on a green leaf
baby.caption: Ladybug larvae look like tiny, spiky alligators and have a massive appetite for aphids.

family.fileTitle: File:Bombus terrestris and Coccinella septempunctata on Cirsium vulgare A 2010-08-02 HPB.jpg
family.alt: A ladybug and a bumblebee feeding on the same purple thistle flower
family.caption: Ladybugs are solitary, but they often gather in large groups in autumn to stay warm.

range.alt: A ladybug climbing on green plant leaves
range.caption: The seven-spotted ladybug is native to Europe and Asia, and was introduced to North America.

size.alt: A ladybug on a leaf showing its small round body size
size.caption: Ladybugs are very small, growing to only about 0.3 inches (7 to 10 mm) long.

closeup.alt: Detailed close-up of a ladybug's glossy black head and red wing cover
closeup.caption: Their bright red and black coloration warns predators that they taste bitter and are toxic.

fun-fact.alt: A ladybug crawling along a plant stem in Yellowstone National Park
fun-fact.caption: A single ladybug can eat up to 5,000 aphids during its lifetime!

core-habitat.alt: A ladybug resting on a green leaf in a meadow
core-habitat.caption: Gardens and farms with flowering plants are ideal habitats for ladybugs.

core-diet.fileTitle: File:The Aphid Party is Over! Attack of the Ladybug.jpg
core-diet.alt: A ladybug hunting on a plant leaf covered in aphids
core-diet.caption: Farmers love ladybugs because they protect crops from sap-sucking pests.

core-family.alt: Two ladybugs side by side on a green leaf
core-family.caption: Ladybugs are solitary beetles that hunt alone during the spring and summer.

core-baby.fileTitle: File:Newly-hatched small larva of Coccinella septempunctata.jpg
core-baby.alt: Newly hatched tiny ladybug larvae on a leaf next to yellow egg shells
core-baby.caption: Females lay clusters of tiny, yellow eggs under leaves near aphid colonies.

core-range.alt: A ladybug on a plant stem in a grassy field
core-range.caption: Ladybugs are highly successful beetles found in many regions globally.
